officialSources.title: NHS App web integration
borrowedRules: web integrations surface a responsive website in the NHS App and must follow the NHS digital service manual
eligibility includes patient-facing personalised services, commissioning, NHS login integration or approval, and meeting NHS App integration standards
integration progresses through expression of interest, product assessment, Sandpit, AOS, SCAL, connection agreement, limited release, full release, and post-go-live assurance
minor jump-off content changes need Integration Manager notification and significant journey changes need product assessment lead time

officialSources.title: Standards for NHS App integration
borrowedRules: minimum accessibility standard is WCAG 2.2 AA
all 17 points of the NHS service standard apply
clinical safety evidence must cover DCB0129 and DCB0160
SCAL evidence must cover clinical safety and data privacy standards

officialSources.title: NHS App integration: expression of interest form
borrowedRules: expression of interest asks whether the product is patient-facing, commissioned, free to patients, and supported by a public-facing service desk
candidate service areas include medical query, admin query, appointments, medicines, messaging, library, and personal health records

officialSources.title: Web Integration Overview
borrowedRules: NHS App hosts supplier pages in a tailored webview with native navigation
NHS App custom user agent lets suppliers hide headers and show NHS App-specific components
jump-off points are in logged-in pages and visibility is decided through rules such as ODS code

officialSources.title: Web Integration Guidance
borrowedRules: suppliers provide base URL per environment and one or more journey paths
NHS App redirects to base URL plus journey path with an assertedLoginIdentity token query parameter
NHS login authorize should use prompt=none and suppliers must handle OIDC error states
site links require coordination with the NHS App onboarding team
conventional file download does not work in web integrations, so artifact delivery needs a bridge-aware contract

officialSources.title: Accessibility guidance for testing
borrowedRules: accessibility testing should combine testing with people, automated testing, and manual testing
keyboard, focus, zoom, page title, headings, labels, colour contrast, and alternative text checks are release-relevant
services must remain responsive and usable at 400 percent zoom without horizontal scrolling or overlapping content

acceptedInto374: baseUrlsByEnvironment and JourneyPathDefinition mirror the developer guidance requirement for base URL and journey paths
JumpOffMapping includes NHS App placement plus ODS visibility and cohort rules
IntegrationEvidencePack requires UX, clinical safety, privacy, SCAL, and incident runbook evidence
ServiceDeskProfile is required because onboarding asks about public-facing service desk support
the route inventory excludes raw download and print assumptions until bridge-aware artifact handling is proven

rejectedOrDeferred.claim: local manifest approval equals NHS App team approval
rejectedOrDeferred.reason: official process requires product assessment, Sandpit, AOS, SCAL, incident rehearsal, connection agreement, and go-live approval

rejectedOrDeferred.claim: route existence is enough for NHS App exposure
rejectedOrDeferred.reason: developer guidance separates jump-off placement, ODS visibility, base URL, and journey paths

rejectedOrDeferred.claim: file downloads and browser exits can use ordinary web behavior
rejectedOrDeferred.reason: developer guidance notes webview limitations; Phase 7 needs bridge-aware artifact and outbound navigation contracts

rejectedOrDeferred.claim: expression of interest is equivalent to partner onboarding completion
rejectedOrDeferred.reason: EOI only starts consideration and review

openExternalDependencies: NHS App Product Guidance document is provided during product assessment and is not represented as already obtained
NHS App team Sandpit and AOS sign-off are not represented as complete
SCAL and connection agreement evidence remains future partner-facing work
